takeaways: Senior housing occupancy hit a new high of 89.9% in primary markets with 19 straight quarters of positive absorption — 2026 projected to break 90% and push toward 93% by 2028.
Welltower paid $87.2M for a 377-unit Florida assisted living asset and secured a $6.25B revolver, signaling REIT capital is back and aggressive on senior housing operators.
Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75% with rate-cut odds pushed to September/October — 10-year Treasury anchored at 4.00%–4.25% sets the refi math for the rest of 2026.

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy hits 89.9%, 19th straight quarter of absorption
items.summary: NIC MAP data shows primary-market senior housing occupancy reached 89.9% and secondary markets hit 90.0% in Q4 2025, with independent living above 90% for the first time since 2019 and assisted living at 87.2%. Rolling four-quarter transaction volume closed at $24B, the highest since Q2 2015, setting a favorable tailwind for Lloyd Jones acquisitions and lease-up underwriting.

items.headline: Welltower pays $87.2M for 377-unit Florida AL after $6.25B revolver
items.summary: Welltower closed on The Arbor at Lake Worth (377 units) in Palm Beach County for $87.2M — roughly $231K/unit — one month after securing a $6.25B revolving credit facility. The REIT remains an aggressive bidder in Florida and Texas senior housing, pushing pricing expectations higher for operator-owners considering dispositions.

items.headline: Fed minutes reveal more officials eyeing possible hikes in 2026
items.summary: April 8 FOMC minutes showed a growing minority of Federal Reserve officials would consider rate hikes this year as oil-driven inflation risks from the Iran conflict complicate the outlook. Markets now price near-zero odds of a cut at the April 29 meeting, with the first cut pushed to September/October — extending borrowing cost pressure on CRE refinancings and development deals.

items.headline: CRE debt capital flows rebound; sales volume forecast up 15–20%
items.summary: Colliers forecasts a 15–20% increase in CRE sales volume in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital re-enter, while Marcus & Millichap reports stronger lending activity with inflation stabilizing near 3%. Conventional commercial mortgage rates sit in a 5.08%–8.75% band with the 10-year Treasury anchored at 4.00%–4.25%, creating a workable (if tight) refinance window for senior housing operators.

items.headline: U.S. News names 1,614 Best Senior Living communities for 2026
items.summary: U.S. News & World Report published its 2026 Best Senior Living ratings today, evaluating 3,000+ communities across independent living, assisted living, memory care and CCRCs and naming 1,614 winners. The ratings drive consumer decision-making for Lloyd Jones communities and are a direct lead-generation channel for operators who make the list.

items.headline: Senior living cost hikes: AL $5,419/mo, memory care $6,690/mo
items.summary: A Place for Mom's 2026 cost report puts median independent living at $3,200/month, assisted living at $5,419, memory care at $6,690, and home care at $34/hour. Rent growth of 3–6% is forecast across senior housing for 2026, with higher-acuity supply-constrained markets at the top of the range — a pricing tailwind for NOI growth.

items.headline: Agentic AI moves from pilot to production in proptech
items.summary: ICSC and MRI research show 97% of brokerage leaders report agent-level AI adoption as agentic AI takes over procurement, pricing, tenant relationship management, and lease drafting. The global AI-in-real-estate market is projected to grow from $303B in 2025 to $989B by 2029 — creating pressure on mid-sized operators like Lloyd Jones to pick a vendor stack now or fall behind on operating margins.

items.headline: Deloitte 2026: AI targets lease drafting, TRM, portfolio mgmt
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 CRE outlook pegs tenant relationship management, lease drafting and portfolio management as the highest-ROI AI use cases, with monster-round proptech financings reappearing for category leaders. Workflow-layer platforms that sit across data and property management tools are emerging as the consolidation point for AI deployment.

items.headline: Proptech's 2026 buzzword is 'workflow' — vertical operator AI
items.summary: Commercial Observer reports the hottest proptech category is vertical, operator-led 'workflow' AI that threads across the real estate lifecycle rather than point solutions. Investors are paying premium prices for companies with durable scale and distribution — a signal that operators should evaluate partners who control the workflow, not just the model.

items.headline: 71% of leaders report higher stress; 40% considering exit
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership trends report finds 71% of leaders under increased stress, 40% considering leaving, and 77% of CHROs lacking confidence in bench strength for critical roles. Bench-strength and manager-stress dynamics are acute for senior housing operators where ED turnover directly drives occupancy and NOI.

items.headline: Microsoft: AI scaling winners anchor to business outcomes, not tools
items.summary: Microsoft's April 1 report on enterprise AI transformation argues the leaders gaining traction are those linking AI deployment to specific P&L outcomes — revenue, retention, cost — rather than running disjointed pilots. Recommended framework for operators: pick 2-3 measurable outcomes (collections, move-in conversion, staffing) before choosing tools.

items.headline: Strategic problem-solving now a top hiring priority for 90% of leaders
items.summary: Business-leadership research shows 90% of senior leaders now consider strategic problem-solving a top hiring and promotion criterion, with emotional intelligence identified as the throughline that keeps teams steady enough to absorb new tools. 'Labor hoarding' of A-players is reframed as strategic retention for 2026.

items.headline: 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life-Wellness report finds 89% of employees say prioritizing wellbeing drives higher performance, but only 54% of workers rate their wellbeing as good — down from 63% in 2024. Workplace wellness programs are shifting from perks to productivity levers, with clear implications for caregiver retention in senior housing.

items.headline: Longevity goes mainstream: metabolic, sleep, strength, community
items.summary: Global Wellness Summit's 2026 trends report frames longevity as an integrated lifestyle combining metabolic optimization, functional nutrition, strength training, restorative sleep, and community connection — a direct programming playbook for senior living resident engagement and active-adult amenity design.

items.headline: Emotional fitness emerges as 2026's core productivity metric
items.summary: Dr. Axe and Who What Wear wellness reports flag 'emotional fitness' as the defining 2026 trend, combining cognitive performance, resilience training, and AI-assisted coaching. Hyper-personalized, biomarker-driven wellness programs are entering mass adoption — early opportunity for operators to differentiate amenity offerings.
